subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — Middle of Year (MOY) Test 2
instructions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer. For type-in questions, write only the number.

questions.questionText: What is 6 × 9?
questions.explanation: 6 groups of 9 is 54.

questions.questionText: The dots below are arranged in equal rows. How many dots are there in all?
questions.explanation: 5 rows of 4 is 5 × 4 = 20 dots.

questions.questionText: What is 42 ÷ 7?
questions.explanation: 42 shared into 7 equal groups is 6 in each group.

questions.questionText: Which multiplication fact matches the array shown below?
questions.explanation: There are 4 rows with 8 dots each, so 4 × 8 = 32.

questions.questionText: Jordan has 54 trading cards. He puts them into 6 equal stacks. How many cards are in each stack?
questions.explanation: 54 ÷ 6 = 9 cards in each stack.

questions.questionText: Select all the expressions that have the same value as 8 × 5.
questions.explanation: 8 × 5 = 40. 5 × 8 = 40 (order does not change the product), and 8 + 8 + 8 + 8 + 8 = 40 (five groups of 8). But 8 × 4 = 32 and 8 + 5 = 13, so those are not correct.

questions.questionText: A baker has 56 muffins. She packs the same number into each of 8 boxes. How many muffins are in each box? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: 56 ÷ 8 = 7 muffins in each box.

questions.questionText: A classroom has 7 rows of desks with 4 desks in each row. Then 6 more desks are brought in. How many desks are there now?
questions.explanation: First 7 × 4 = 28 desks. Then 28 + 6 = 34 desks.

questions.questionText: Mia buys 5 bags of apples with 9 apples in each bag. She uses 8 apples to make a pie. How many apples does she have left? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: First 5 × 9 = 45 apples. Then 45 − 8 = 37 apples left.

questions.questionText: Look at the pattern: 4, 8, 12, 16, ___. The numbers increase by 4 each time. Which statement is true about every number in this pattern?
questions.explanation: Every multiple of 4 is even, so all the numbers in the pattern are even.

questions.questionText: Select all the number sentences that equal 36.
questions.explanation: 6 × 6 = 36, 4 × 9 = 36, and 3 × 12 = 36. But 7 × 5 = 35, so it is not correct.

questions.questionText: What fraction does the point show on the number line?
questions.explanation: The number line is split into 3 equal parts. The point is on the 1st mark, so it is 1/3.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the bar is shaded?
questions.explanation: The bar has 8 equal parts and 5 are shaded, so 5/8 is shaded.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the circle is shaded?
questions.explanation: The circle has 4 equal parts and 3 are shaded, so 3/4 is shaded.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equal to 1/3?
questions.explanation: Bar A shows 1/3 shaded and Bar B shows 2/6 shaded with the same amount, so 2/6 = 1/3.

questions.questionText: Two number lines are the same length. On the first, a point sits at 1/3. On the second, a point sits at 1/4. Which statement is true?
questions.explanation: When a whole is split into 3 parts, each part is bigger than when it is split into 4 parts. So 1/3 is greater than 1/4.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equal to the whole number 1?
questions.explanation: When the top number and bottom number are the same, the fraction equals one whole. So 4/4 = 1.

questions.questionText: There are 12 marbles. One third (1/3) of them are blue. How many marbles are blue? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: To find 1/3 of 12, divide 12 into 3 equal groups: 12 ÷ 3 = 4 blue marbles.

questions.questionText: Select all the fractions that are equal to 1/2.
questions.explanation: 3/6, 4/8, and 5/10 all equal 1/2. But 2/3 is more than 1/2, so it is not correct.

questions.questionText: Each square is 1 square unit. What is the area of the rectangle?
questions.explanation: Area = length × width = 6 × 2 = 12 square units.

questions.questionText: What is the perimeter of the rectangle?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 7 + 3 + 7 + 3 = 20 cm.

questions.questionText: A rectangle is 9 cm long and 2 cm wide. Which statement is true about its area and perimeter?
options: The area is 18 sq cm and the perimeter is 22 cm
The area is 22 sq cm and the perimeter is 18 cm
The area and perimeter are both 18
The area is 18 sq cm and the perimeter is 11 cm
questions.explanation: Area = 9 × 2 = 18 square cm. Perimeter = 9 + 2 + 9 + 2 = 22 cm.

questions.questionText: A poster is 7 inches wide and 5 inches tall. What is the area of the poster in square inches? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: Area = 7 × 5 = 35 square inches.

questions.questionText: What time does the clock show?
questions.explanation: The minute hand points to 6, which is 30 minutes. The hour hand is halfway between 6 and 7, so the time is 6:30.

questions.questionText: Soccer practice starts at 4:50 and lasts 30 minutes. What time does practice end?
questions.explanation: From 4:50, add 10 minutes to reach 5:00, then 20 more minutes to reach 5:20. So practice ends at 5:20.

questions.questionText: Art class begins at 1:25 and ends at 2:05. How many minutes long is the class? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: From 1:25 to 2:00 is 35 minutes, then 5 more minutes to 2:05 makes 40 minutes.

questions.questionText: The bar graph shows how many cans each grade collected. How many more cans did Grade 3 collect than Grade 1?
questions.explanation: Grade 3 collected 25 cans and Grade 1 collected 15 cans. 25 − 15 = 10 more cans.

questions.questionText: Each ⭐ stands for 4 stickers. How many more stickers did Lee earn than Sam?
questions.explanation: Lee has 6 stars = 6 × 4 = 24 stickers. Sam has 3 stars = 3 × 4 = 12 stickers. 24 − 12 = 12 more stickers.

questions.questionText: What number do the base-ten blocks show?
questions.explanation: 4 hundreds, 0 tens, and 7 ones make 400 + 0 + 7 = 407.

questions.questionText: What is 549 rounded to the nearest hundred?
questions.explanation: The halfway point between 500 and 600 is 550. Since 549 is just below 550, it rounds down to 500.
